title: Mastering Minecraft Commands
description: A complete step-by-step guide to building commands from scratch with full explanations, structure, permissions, tab completion, and debugging.

lessons.title: Command Basics (FULL SETUP)
lessons.content:
# Command Basics (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Open Your Project

Open IntelliJ
→ Open your plugin project

You will work in TWO places:
1. plugin.yml
2. Java classes

---

## Step 2: Register Command (plugin.yml)

Open:
src/main/resources/plugin.yml

Add:

```yml
commands:
  hello:
    description: Says hello
```

### Breakdown:
commands:
→ section for commands

hello:
→ command name (/hello)

---

## Step 3: Create Command Class

Create new file:
HelloCommand.java

Path:
src/main/java/your/package/HelloCommand.java

```java
public class HelloCommand implements CommandExecutor {

    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        sender.sendMessage("Hello, world!");
        return true;
    }
}
```

---

## Step 4: FULL Breakdown

public class HelloCommand
→ defines class

implements CommandExecutor
→ tells Spigot this class handles commands

onCommand(...)
→ THIS method runs when command is used

CommandSender sender
→ who ran command

String[] args
→ arguments

return true;
→ tells server command worked

---

## Step 5: Register Command in Main Class

Open Main.java

Inside onEnable():

```java
getCommand("hello").setExecutor(new HelloCommand());
```

### Breakdown:
getCommand("hello")
→ finds command from plugin.yml

setExecutor(...)
→ assigns class to run it

---

## Step 6: Run & Test

1. Start server
2. Join Minecraft
3. Run:
/hello

Expected:
Hello, world!

---

## Step 7: Common Errors

❌ Command not in plugin.yml
❌ Forgot setExecutor
❌ Spelling mismatch

---

## Mini Challenge
Change message to "Welcome!"

## Recap
plugin.yml → register
class → logic
main → connect

lessons.title: Command Sender & Player Checks
lessons.content:
# Command Sender (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Problem

Commands can be run by:
- Players
- Console

Console has NO:
- location
- inventory

---

## Step 2: Code

```java
if (!(sender instanceof Player)) {
    sender.sendMessage("Only players can use this!");
    return true;
}

Player player = (Player) sender;
```

---

## Step 3: FULL Breakdown

instanceof
→ checks type

!(...)
→ NOT

(Player) sender
→ casting (converting type)

---

## Step 4: Why This Matters

If you skip this:
→ server crashes when console runs command

---

## Step 5: What Happens

Console runs → blocked
Player runs → allowed

---

## Mini Challenge
Send player their name

## Recap
Always check before casting

lessons.title: Permissions (FULL GUIDE)
lessons.content:
# Permissions (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: plugin.yml

```yml
commands:
  hello:
    permission: myplugin.hello
```

---

## Step 2: Code Check

```java
if (!sender.hasPermission("myplugin.hello")) {
    sender.sendMessage("No permission!");
    return true;
}
```

---

## Step 3: Breakdown

hasPermission(...)
→ checks permission

! → NOT

---

## Step 4: Where This Goes

TOP of command method

---

## Step 5: What Happens

No permission → blocked

---

## Mini Challenge
Change permission name

## Recap
Always protect commands

lessons.title: Tab Completion (FULL GUIDE)
lessons.content:
# Tab Completion (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Create Class

HelloTab.java

## Step 2: Code

```java
public class HelloTab implements TabCompleter {

    @Override
    public List<String> onTabComplete(CommandSender sender, Command command, String alias, String[] args) {

        if (args.length == 1) {
            return Arrays.asList("Steve", "Alex");
        }

        return Collections.emptyList();
    }
}
```

---

## Step 3: Register

```java
getCommand("hello").setTabCompleter(new HelloTab());
```

---

## Step 4: What Happens

Type:
/hello <TAB>

→ shows suggestions

---

## Step 5: Why Useful

- Helps users
- Prevents mistakes

---

## Mini Challenge
Add more names

## Recap
Tab = suggestions

lessons.title: Advanced Commands (Subcommands)
lessons.content:
# Subcommands (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Structure

```java
if (args.length == 0) {
    sender.sendMessage("/hello reload");
    return true;
}

if (args[0].equalsIgnoreCase("reload")) {
    sender.sendMessage("Reloaded!");
}
```

---

## Step 2: Breakdown

args[0]
→ decides action

.equalsIgnoreCase
→ ignores capital letters

---

## Step 3: Why This Is Powerful

You can create:
/hello give
/hello reload
/hello info

---

## Mini Challenge
Add /hello help

## Recap
args[0] controls command system

lessons.title: Testing & Debugging
lessons.content:
# Debugging Commands (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Add Logs

```java
getLogger().info("Command executed");
```

---

## Step 2: Test Process

1. Run command
2. Check console
3. Fix errors

---

## Step 3: Common Errors

- Not in plugin.yml
- Not registered
- Typo in name

---

## Step 4: Important Rule

return false;
→ shows usage from plugin.yml

return true;
→ silent success

---

## Mini Challenge
Add debug message with args

## Recap
Debug = logs + testing
